data.title: U.S. Congress to Discuss CLARITY Act's Impact on Stablecoin Revenue
data.description: The U.S. Congress is preparing for its third meeting concerning the CLARITY Act, which addresses the structure of the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, this session is scheduled for February 20 at 2:00 p.m. UTC and will focus on the revenue generated by stablecoins. The meeting will feature key participants from both the cryptocurrency and banking sectors.

data.title: Landslide Strikes Indonesia's Major Nickel Smelting Complex
data.description: A landslide has occurred at the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park, one of the largest nickel smelting complexes globally. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this incident is the latest in a series of accidents affecting the facility. The industrial park, known for its significant contribution to nickel production, has faced multiple challenges recently, raising concerns about safety and operational continuity. The impact of the landslide on production and the broader nickel market remains to be seen, as authorities assess the situation and work towards mitigating further risks.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank to Increase Policy Decision Frequency Starting 2027
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand has announced a change in its policy decision schedule, increasing the number of annual meetings from seven to eight starting in 2027. According to Jin10, this adjustment is in response to the shift in the release of Consumer Price Index (CPI) data from quarterly to monthly beginning next year. To accommodate the new schedule, the February 2027 decision date will be moved forward by one week.

Although the central bank has set its decision dates through February 2028, the Monetary Policy Committee retains the ability to make unscheduled decisions if financial or economic conditions warrant. Analysts have noted that this change is procedural rather than directional. However, the increased frequency of meetings may slightly reduce policy lag effects and could heighten the sensitivity of the New Zealand dollar and interest rates to unexpected changes in monthly CPI data.

data.title: Denmark Faces Economic Challenges Amid Corporate Layoffs
data.description: Denmark's economy appears robust, yet recent mass layoffs at major companies like Novo Nordisk have raised concerns about potential further job cuts. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of these layoffs on the Danish workforce. Despite positive economic indicators, the reality of job losses is prompting a reevaluation of the country's economic stability. As companies reassess their workforce needs, the threat of additional layoffs looms, challenging the perception of Denmark's economic strength.

data.title: US CLARITY Act to pass 'hopefully by April': Senator Bernie Moreno
data.description:
Odds of the US CLARITY Act passing in 2026 briefly spike to 90% on Polymarket on Wednesday amid optimistic comments from US Senator Bernie Moreno.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-us-clarity-act-coinbase-brian-armstrong-bernie-moreno?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: US Senator: Clarity Act May Pass in April; Coinbase Says "Pathway" Emerges
data.description: U.S. Senator Bernie Moreno stated that the Clarisity Act, a bill structuring the crypto market, is expected to pass Congress before April. In an interview with CNBC, Moreno said the bill's progress has a clear path forward. Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, in the same interview, stated that while there were previous disagreements regarding stablecoin interest terms and regulatory jurisdiction, all parties are now seeking a "win-win" solution. Reports indicate that stablecoin yield terms are one of the main points of contention in the bill's progress. Moreno also stated that he does not believe the midterm election results will affect the bill's passage. Previously, White House officials also expressed positive expectations for the bill's progress earlier this year. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: North Korean Leader Kim Jong Un Attends Rocket Launcher Ceremony in Pyongyang
data.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un participated in a ceremony on February 18 in Pyongyang, where a 600mm rocket launcher was presented to the Workers' Party of Korea's Ninth Congress. According to Ming Pao, Kim Jong Un delivered a speech at the event and personally drove the rocket launcher vehicle to inspect the weaponry. He stated that the 600mm rocket launcher would enhance North Korea's war deterrence capabilities and fulfill strategic objectives.

data.title: Wu Jiezhuang: Hong Kong will issue the first batch of stablecoin issuer licenses in March, and suggests promoting their use through airdrops.
data.description: Hong Kong Legislative Council member Ng Kit-chung stated on the "Financial Chat" program that Hong Kong is expected to issue its first batch of stablecoin issuer licenses in March. He suggested that after the relevant institutions obtain licenses, the government could issue stablecoin-based consumption vouchers to eligible citizens for use by local SMEs, thereby promoting the application of stablecoins in Hong Kong. Ng pointed out that this move could draw on the experience of promoting electronic payments through electronic consumption vouchers, stimulating the local economy through stablecoin usage scenarios and enhancing Hong Kong's development positioning in the Web3 and digital asset fields. He stated that the relevant administrative costs could be borne by the licensed institutions. (Techub News)

data.title: Federal Reserve researchers say Kalshi data can provide real-time reference for policymaking.
data.description: Three researchers from the Federal Reserve System published a paper on February 12th arguing that data from the prediction market platform Kalshi can reflect macroeconomic expectations more in real time and should be included in the Fed's decision-making framework. The authors include Fed Chief Economist Anthony Diercks, research assistant Jared Dean Katz, and Johns Hopkins University researcher Jonathan Wright. The study compared Kalshi data with traditional surveys and market-implied expectations, concluding that Kalshi provides high-frequency, continuously updated probability distributions for measuring the risk-neutral probability of macroeconomic indicators such as interest rate decisions. The researchers noted that Kalshi reacts more quickly after major policy statements or economic data releases. However, the report emphasizes that the Fed's research paper is for internal discussion and does not represent a formal policy stance. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: The White House will hold its third stablecoin yield meeting tomorrow.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett posted on the X platform that the third stablecoin yield conference is scheduled to be held at the White House tomorrow morning at 9:00 AM Eastern Time. A small group of representatives from the cryptocurrency industry and the banking sector is expected to attend.

data.title: White House to Host Third Stablecoin Yield Meeting
data.description: Eleanor Terrett posted on X. A third meeting focused on stablecoin yields is scheduled to occur at the White House tomorrow at 9:00 A.M. ET. The meeting will include a select group of representatives from the cryptocurrency and banking sectors. Further details are anticipated to be released in the morning.

data.title: Landslide at Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park Results in Fatality
data.description: A landslide occurred at the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park on Wednesday, resulting in the death of one worker. Bloomberg posted on X, citing information from IMIP spokesperson Dedy Kurniawan. The incident highlights ongoing safety concerns in industrial areas. Further details about the cause of the landslide and measures being taken to prevent future occurrences have not been disclosed.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 610 USDT with a Narrowed 1.25% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 19, 2026, 04:32 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 610 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 610.119995 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.25% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: XRP Demand Defies Bear Market Predictions
data.description: Crypto KOL Eli5defi on X highlights the unexpected demand for XRP despite predictions of a bear market. Contrary to earlier claims that XRP lacked demand, recent developments suggest otherwise, challenging the notion of an impending bear market.

data.title: OpenAI Collaborates with Tata Group on AI Technologies in India
data.description: OpenAI has announced a partnership with the Tata Group to advance artificial intelligence technologies in India. Bloomberg posted on X that this collaboration includes the development of data center infrastructure, which has the potential to become one of the largest in the country. The partnership aims to leverage Tata Group's extensive experience in technology and infrastructure to enhance AI capabilities and support the growing demand for AI solutions in India. This move is expected to bolster India's position in the global AI landscape, fostering innovation and technological growth.

data.title: India's AI Ambitions Explored by Google DeepMind CEO
data.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis shared insights on India's potential to become a leader in artificial intelligence during a discussion with Bloomberg TV. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Hassabis's views on India's strategic approach to AI development. Hassabis emphasized the importance of innovation and collaboration in advancing AI technologies, noting India's growing influence in the global tech landscape. The conversation took place amid India's AI Summit, where industry leaders are gathering to discuss the future of AI and its impact on various sectors. Hassabis's remarks underscore the significance of India's role in shaping AI advancements and the opportunities for growth in this field.

data.title: Bill Gates Withdraws from Indian AI Summit Keynote
data.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates unexpectedly canceled his keynote address at a prominent AI summit in India just hours before he was scheduled to speak. Bloomberg posted on X that Gates' withdrawal from the event was announced without a detailed explanation for the sudden change in plans. The summit, which gathers leading figures in artificial intelligence, was set to feature Gates as a key speaker, highlighting the importance of AI in global technological advancements. The organizers have yet to comment on the impact of his absence on the event's proceedings.

data.title: Hong Kong to Issue Stablecoin Licenses in March to Boost Crypto Adoption
data.description:
Hong Kong legislator Ng Kit Chuang announced on February 19 that the city will issue its first batch of stablecoin licenses in March. According to BlockBeats, Ng expressed hope that the government would encourage the use of cryptocurrencies among Hong Kong residents through stablecoin airdrops.

Ng highlighted that three years ago, the government distributed electronic consumption vouchers worth HKD 10,000 to each citizen, significantly popularizing electronic payments. He suggested that once stablecoin issuers receive their licenses, the government could distribute stablecoin airdrops to eligible citizens for spending at local small and medium-sized enterprises, such as restaurants, entertainment venues, and cinemas. This initiative aims to enhance Hong Kong's status as a Web3 and digital asset hub, achieving both economic stimulation and increased cryptocurrency adoption.

Ng emphasized that this approach is not merely a government handout but a strategy to leverage stablecoin consumption vouchers to create a multiplier effect. The administrative costs would be covered by the licensed companies.

data.title: Japan's Proposed Food Tax Cut Raises Concerns Among Economists
data.description: A recent Reuters survey reveals that 57% of economists express varying levels of concern over Japan's proposed two-year food consumption tax reduction plan. According to Jin10, the initiative aims to alleviate financial pressure on households, but experts worry about its potential impact on government revenue and economic stability. The plan has sparked debate among policymakers and economists, highlighting the challenges of balancing fiscal policy with consumer relief efforts.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Economists Predict Japan's Intervention in Currency Market to Curb Yen Weakness
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Reuters reveals that 69% of economists anticipate Japan's intervention in the currency market to address the weakening yen. According to Jin10, the survey indicates that the USD/JPY exchange rate reaching 160 could likely trigger such intervention. The economists' consensus highlights concerns over the yen's depreciation and its potential impact on Japan's economy. The survey underscores the growing expectation for governmental action to stabilize the currency and mitigate adverse economic effects.
